stimulus.content: In 1607, English settlers founded Jamestown in Virginia. The Virginia Company of London paid for the trip. The company hoped the settlers would find gold and other valuable resources to send back to England.

stem: Based on this passage, what was the main economic reason the Virginia Company funded the colony?

choices.text: To set up schools for settlers
To earn profit from new resources
To build a strong colonial army
To create a new form of government

answer_explanation: The Virginia Company funded the Jamestown colony primarily to make a profit. The company expected settlers to find gold and other valuable resources that could be sent back to England and sold. Economic gain was the main motivation for many early colonial ventures.
